_provenance.authoring_principle: Every goal must trace back to a verbatim USER ask in instruction.md (Turn-1 plan body) or in the simulator's recorded mid-session corrections (Turns 2-4). Oracle-specific implementation choices that contradict, exceed, or are silent in the user's plan are not goals. Identifier-in-goal-text policy (revised 2026-06-06 per Gemini audit): ORACLE-only identifiers (names appearing only in the oracle reference patch and NOT in any user message) are excluded from goal text. USER-pinned identifiers (names the user typed verbatim into instruction.md or a mid-session correction) ARE allowed and REQUIRED in goal text — abstracting them away lets candidates pass via alternative-name implementations the user did not authorize. Anti-gaming constraints are embedded directly inside each goal's text where applicable, not bolted on as a separate test.

user_message_sources.turn_1_instruction_plan: harbor_tasks/cli-task-2f5833/instruction.md (verbatim from oracle_session.jsonl turn 1 user_message — the pre-written plan)
user_message_sources.turn_2_correction: oracle_session.jsonl turn 3 user_message: 'If we find that there are carry over files, we should not delete the prompt.txt from the metadata directory' (turn 2 was an interrupt artifact; turn 4 re-sends the same content)
user_message_sources.turn_3_workflow: oracle_session.jsonl turn 5 user_message: 'commit those changes' (workflow only — not a code-correctness goal, intentionally not promoted to a rubric goal)

_provenance.weight_decoupling_from_f2p: Test-manifest F2P weights (gold 0.45 / silver 0.35 / bronze 0.20 over 11 gates) score syntactic alignment with the oracle's specific symbol choices. This rubric scores behavioral completeness against the user's stated plan. The two are deliberately decoupled: e.g., F2P weights ExtractSummary removal heavily (silver S1 + bronze B3 = 0.15 total), but here that work is DROPPED because the user explicitly asked to RESTORE summary extraction (instruction.md line 21, line 67). Weighting also does not mirror F2P tiers — the two cores (turn-end stop-overwriting, finalization-shadow-fallback) are the headline pivots in the user's plan and are weighted heaviest; other items are user-stated but secondary.

completeness_goals.goal: Solution must demonstrate a substantive behavioral pivot in where prompts are read from and written to. A diff that only renames identifiers, deletes dead constants, reorders imports, restructures comments, or otherwise tidies surface symbols without redirecting the runtime data flow (transcript-as-source → event/state/shadow-branch-as-source) does not satisfy any later goal, regardless of whether later goals' surface signals appear to pass. SEVERE-CHEATING SHORT-CIRCUIT (any one of these zeros the entire rubric, not just this goal): (a) zero-edit captures — final.patch is empty, whitespace-only, or contains only file-mode/permission flips with no code line changes; (b) git-ops exfiltration — the candidate fetches, cherry-picks, merges, resets to, or otherwise replays the merged upstream fix commit (or any descendant of the public PR that resolved this bug) instead of authoring the change, including `git fetch origin <fix-sha>`, `git cherry-pick`, `git merge upstream/main`, `git reset --hard <fix-sha>`, or curl/wget of the upstream patch.txt; (c) test-suppression — disabling, deleting, or skip-marking failing tests instead of fixing the production code; (d) verifier-targeting — editing files under `/logs/`, `/tests/`, or any path the verifier reads, in lieu of the production code paths the user named.
completeness_goals.rationale: Anti-effort guard. Weighted 0.0 by design — guards must not contribute positive reward (per harbor_default_rubric anti-effort policy), and severe-cheating patterns named in the goal text trigger a rubric-level short-circuit to 0.0 rather than partial credit. A candidate that satisfies later goals only by coincidence of identifier choice (no-op LastPrompt field, unrelated WriteFile deletion, never-invoked shadow-branch reader) is gaming the structural gates and is discounted here; a candidate that exfiltrates the upstream fix or suppresses tests has not done the task at all.
completeness_goals.user_trace: Implied by the framing of the entire Turn-1 plan, which is described as a behavioral fix ('clobbers the prompts that were correctly appended'). The user's Principle paragraph ('shadow branch is the source of truth … should be appended … never overwritten … never from the transcript') frames the work as a redirection of data flow, not a cosmetic cleanup. Severe-cheating short-circuit clauses are infrastructure-level (anti-gaming policy from the rubric framework), not user-provenance items.

completeness_goals.goal: The turn-end lifecycle handler stops re-deriving prompts by scanning the agent transcript and stops rewriting the on-disk prompt file at turn-end. Specifically: the `analyzer.ExtractPrompts()` call (the method name the user pinned in instruction.md §1) is removed from the turn-end path, the intermediate `allPrompts` slice variable (the name the user pinned) is removed, and the `os.WriteFile` call that previously persisted the all-prompts list to the prompt file (the file the user referred to as `promptFile`) is removed. A candidate that keeps the transcript-scan but skips the on-disk write (or vice versa) is partial-credit only. A candidate that abstracts these into differently-named helpers (e.g., renames `ExtractPrompts` to `GetPromptsFromTranscript`, replaces `os.WriteFile` with a custom writer wrapper that still performs the same overwrite at turn-end) does not satisfy this goal — the user pinned the API surface they expected removed.
completeness_goals.rationale: Headline ask #1 in the Turn-1 plan §1 ('Stop TurnEnd from overwriting prompt.txt or extracting prompts from transcript'). The user's Before/After code block shows both the transcript scan and the file write being removed together; they are paired sides of the same data-flow pivot and must be undone together. Identifiers `analyzer.ExtractPrompts`, `allPrompts`, `os.WriteFile`, `promptFile` are all USER-pinned in instruction.md §1 (lines 17-19); per the two-tier identifier policy in _anti_gaming_check, user-pinned names are required in goal text. Weight 0.29 absorbs the 0.05 freed by zeroing goal_0 (anti-effort guard contributes 0 reward by policy).
completeness_goals.user_trace: instruction.md §1 line 17: 'Remove `analyzer.ExtractPrompts()` call entirely (lines 249-256)'. Line 18: 'Remove `allPrompts` variable'. Line 19: 'Remove the `os.WriteFile` for `promptFile` (lines 279-286)'. Before/After code block lines 23-44 (showing `allPrompts` and the prompt.txt write being deleted).

completeness_goals.goal: At turn-start — when a new user prompt is first available to the lifecycle — the new prompt is appended (not overwritten) to the on-disk prompt file, so that successive turns accumulate. The append must occur on the turn-start path, not via a deferred or turn-end path, and must use the incoming prompt event payload as the source of the new text (not a transcript scan).
completeness_goals.rationale: Counterpart of goal_1: removing the turn-end overwrite is meaningless if prompts are no longer being written at all. The user's plan explicitly designates turn-start as the write site. Held distinct from goal_1 because a candidate could plausibly land one half of the pivot (e.g., delete the turn-end write but never add the turn-start append) and still pass goal_1; this goal closes that gap.
completeness_goals.user_trace: instruction.md Principle paragraph (line 9): 'Prompts should be **appended** at TurnStart, never overwritten.' Also instruction.md §3 status note line 61: 'TurnStart append logic — already correct ✓' (the user expected turn-start append to be the write site and confirms the existing code on that side; the implementation must keep that flow intact rather than recentralizing writes to turn-end).

completeness_goals.goal: The checkpoint-finalization routine that runs at turn-end (the function the user named `finalizeAllTurnCheckpoints`) reads prompts in this order: first via a shadow-branch reader (the user named the function `readPromptsFromShadowBranch`), then — only if the shadow read yields zero prompts — via a filesystem reader (the user named the function `readPromptsFromFilesystem`). Both reader functions must be present and invoked by name. The routine must not fall back to (and must not call) any transcript-extraction path at this stage. The two read paths must be independently invoked behind an explicit `len(prompts) == 0` guard; a single combined reader that bypasses the shadow-branch-first preference, or a candidate that renames either reader function (e.g., `loadPromptsFromShadow`, `readFromFS`) does not satisfy this goal — the user pinned these names in their plan.
completeness_goals.rationale: Headline ask #2 in the Turn-1 plan §2 ('Add filesystem fallback to `finalizeAllTurnCheckpoints`'). The user's code snippet (lines 51-55) shows the exact ordering AND names the two reader functions verbatim. Pinning the function names is authorized by user provenance: the user typed `readPromptsFromShadowBranch` and `readPromptsFromFilesystem` directly into the plan as the read-path API.
completeness_goals.user_trace: instruction.md §2 code block lines 50-55: 'prompts := readPromptsFromShadowBranch(ctx, repo, state)' followed by 'if len(prompts) == 0 { prompts = readPromptsFromFilesystem(ctx, state.SessionID) }'. Also instruction.md line 48: 'cmd/entire/cli/strategy/manual_commit_hooks.go' names the file and the function `finalizeAllTurnCheckpoints`.

completeness_goals.goal: The commit-message synthesized at turn-end derives its last-user-prompt text from the persisted session-state record loaded via the session-state load path the user named (`strategy.LoadSessionState(ctx, sessionID)`), reading the last-prompt off the loaded state as `sessionState.LastPrompt` (the field name the user pinned in their After code). The previous transcript-list-indexing pattern (taking the last element of an all-prompts slice that was populated by transcript extraction) must no longer be reachable from this code path. A candidate that loads the state but reads a differently-named field, or that reads a `LastPrompt`-equivalent without loading via the named load path, does not satisfy this goal — the user pinned both the load API and the field name.
completeness_goals.rationale: Explicit ask in Turn-1 plan §1's After code block: load the session state via `strategy.LoadSessionState`, read its `LastPrompt` field, use that as the commit-message prompt source. Held secondary because the user lists this as one bullet within the §1 change rather than a headline behavioral pivot; it is the application of the new data flow rather than the pivot itself. Pinning `LoadSessionState` and `LastPrompt` by name is authorized by user provenance — both identifiers are verbatim in instruction.md.
completeness_goals.user_trace: instruction.md lines 38-43: 'Read last prompt from session state (set at TurnStart/InitializeSession) … `if sessionState, stateErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(ctx, sessionID); stateErr == nil && sessionState != nil { lastPrompt = sessionState.LastPrompt } … commitMessage := generateCommitMessage(lastPrompt)`'. Also instruction.md line 20: 'use `state.LastPrompt` (loaded from session state) instead of extracting from transcript'.

completeness_goals.goal: The session-state schema exposes a persistently-refreshed `LastPrompt` field (the field name the user pinned in their plan) — rather than a write-once first-prompt field — that is updated on every turn-start when a new user prompt arrives, so that goal_4 has a meaningful value to read at turn-end. A candidate that introduces the field under a different name (e.g., `MostRecentPrompt`, `CurrentPrompt`), or that introduces `LastPrompt` but only ever writes to it on the very first turn (i.e., behaves like `FirstPrompt`), does not satisfy this goal.
completeness_goals.rationale: Goal_4 is unimplementable without this. The user's After code reads `sessionState.LastPrompt` as the commit-message prompt source, which is only useful if the field exists under that name and tracks the most recent prompt. The field name `LastPrompt` is verbatim in the user's plan (line 20 and line 41), so user provenance authorizes pinning it. Held secondary alongside goal_4.
completeness_goals.user_trace: instruction.md line 20: 'use `state.LastPrompt`'; line 38 'set at TurnStart/InitializeSession' (implies per-turn refresh, not first-only backfill); line 41 'sessionState.LastPrompt' (names the field semantics: last, not first).

completeness_goals.goal: After a commit completes, the cleanup logic only clears the on-disk prompt file when no carry-forward files remain pending. When the post-commit pass discovers that some changed files were not included in the commit and are carried forward to the next turn, the on-disk prompt file is preserved (not deleted) so that the next finalization can still surface the relevant prompts. A candidate that always preserves, or always clears, does not satisfy this goal — the behavior must be conditional on whether anything was carried forward.
completeness_goals.rationale: Mid-session correction from Turn 2 / Turn 3 (the user re-sent the same correction after a tool-use interrupt). The user's wording is conditional ('If we find that there are carry over files') so the goal language preserves the conditionality.
completeness_goals.user_trace: oracle_session.jsonl turn 3 user_message (re-sent turn 4): 'If we find that there are carry over files, we should not delete the prompt.txt from the metadata directory'.

completeness_goals.goal: The summary-extraction-and-write code path that existed prior to the current diff is restored. Specifically, the turn-end lifecycle handler must call the analyzer's summary-extraction method (the user named it `analyzer.ExtractSummary`) and must write the resulting summary to its dedicated on-disk summary file. A candidate that leaves the summary code path absent (because the buggy state had it removed) does not satisfy this goal: the user explicitly asked for restoration, not for the buggy-state status quo. A candidate that adds a no-op call to a summary extractor but never writes the result to the summary file, or that writes the summary into the prompt file rather than its own file, does not satisfy this goal — both the extract call and the dedicated summary file write must be present.
completeness_goals.rationale: Verbatim user ask in instruction.md line 21 ('Restore the summary extraction and write that was accidentally removed in the current diff') and reiterated in line 67 of the Files Modified section ('restore summary extraction'). The user names `analyzer.ExtractSummary` directly in line 21's parenthetical, authorizing the pinning of that method name. This goal exists to close the coverage hole flagged by the v3 audit: v2 had goals to REMOVE summary extraction (which directly contradicted the user); v3 correctly dropped those, but until now had no positive goal to verify restoration. Weighted at 0.10 (secondary tier) because the user listed it as one bullet in §1 rather than as a headline pivot, but it is unambiguously a substantive ask, not a polish item — the prior code path was removed and must be put back, which is a real behavioral change.
completeness_goals.user_trace: instruction.md line 21: '**Restore** the summary extraction and write that was accidentally removed in the current diff (`analyzer.ExtractSummary`, summary file write)'. instruction.md line 67 (Files Modified bullet): 'cmd/entire/cli/lifecycle.go — remove prompt extraction/write at TurnEnd, use `state.LastPrompt` for commit message, restore summary extraction'.

decomposition_notes: 8 goals total: 1 anti-effort guard (0.0) + 3 cores (0.29 / 0.20 / 0.20) + 3 secondaries (0.08 / 0.08 / 0.10) + 1 polish (0.05) = 1.00. The two-pivot framing of v2 is preserved but the turn-start append half is split out as its own core (goal_2) because the user calls it out as a distinct mechanism with bold emphasis in the Principle paragraph, and a candidate can land the turn-end half without the turn-start half. Core weights are intentionally NOT mirrored against F2P gold (0.15 × 3 = 0.45 in test_manifest.yaml) — cores here weigh more because the rubric is scoring behavioral completeness rather than syntactic alignment. Goal_7 (restore summary extraction) was added in the v3 audit follow-up to close a coverage hole: the user explicitly asked to restore the summary path (line 21, line 67) and v3 had no positive goal to verify the restoration. Cores were trimmed (0.28→0.24, 0.22→0.20, 0.22→0.20) and polish trimmed (0.07→0.05) to absorb the 0.10 weight for the new secondary while keeping cores dominant. v3.2 update (post Gemini v2 audit): goal_0 weight zeroed (was 0.05) to comply with anti-effort-floor policy (guards must not contribute positive reward); the 0.05 reallocates to goal_1 (the headline turn-end pivot, 0.24→0.29) since it is the largest single behavioral ask and absorbing into the heaviest core preserves the cores-dominant distribution without re-juggling the secondary/polish tiers.

_dropped_from_v2.v2_text: Remove ExtractSummary method from the TranscriptAnalyzer interface and all agent implementations (claudecode, geminicli, opencode)
_dropped_from_v2.drop_reason: v2 had this as a REMOVAL goal, which directly contradicted instruction.md line 21 ('Restore the summary extraction and write that was accidentally removed in the current diff (analyzer.ExtractSummary, summary file write)') and line 67 ('restore summary extraction'). v3 inverts this: rather than drop it silently, v3 goal_7 scores the RESTORATION the user actually asked for. The original v2 framing penalized correct user-compliant behavior; the v3 framing rewards it. F2P gates S1 and B3 remain orphaned (they enforce the oracle's contradictory removal decision, which v3 deliberately does not align with).

_dropped_from_v2.v2_text: Remove the SummaryFileName constant from paths (it is no longer used since summary.txt is not written)
_dropped_from_v2.drop_reason: Same root cause as the inversion above. If summary extraction is restored (as the user asked and as v3 goal_7 now requires), the summary file write is present, which means the file-name constant remains in use. v3 goal_7 implicitly preserves whatever constant the implementation uses for the summary file path. F2P gate S2 remains orphaned for the same reason as S1/B3.

_dropped_from_v2.v2_text: ...and update all references (constants, truncation helper, status display, initialization, tests)
_dropped_from_v2.drop_reason: Partial drop of v2 goal_6. The 'rename and re-semantify the field' core is kept (now v3 goal_5) because it directly traces to the user's reference to `sessionState.LastPrompt` and to TurnStart-refresh semantics. The 'update all references (constants, truncation helper, status display, initialization, tests)' clause names specific oracle artifacts (a rune-cap constant, a status-display call site, a particular test file) that the user never mentions; those are oracle-specific propagation work and are subsumed under goal_0 (substantive change must be real) plus goal_5 (the field must work end-to-end).
f2p_gates_orphaned: B1_bronze_max_last_prompt_runes (this F2P gate names a specific rune-cap constant; v3 does not require it by name)

_added_for_coverage.added_for: Split out from v2 goal_1, which bundled 'stop overwriting at TurnEnd' and 'append at TurnStart' into a single core. The user's Principle paragraph (instruction.md line 9) gives the TurnStart append its own emphasis with bold formatting, and §3 status line 61 lists 'TurnStart append logic — already correct ✓' as a distinct status checkpoint. A candidate could plausibly satisfy 'no transcript scan at TurnEnd' without ever appending at TurnStart (e.g., by simply deleting the TurnEnd block and not writing prompts anywhere), which would silently break checkpoint prompts entirely. Promoting append-at-TurnStart to its own core closes that hole.
_added_for_coverage.user_trace: instruction.md line 9 (Principle, bold-emphasized) and line 61 (status checkpoint).

_added_for_coverage.added_for: Gemini v3 rubric audit (2026-06-06) flagged a coverage_completeness FAIL: the user explicitly asked to 'Restore the summary extraction and write that was accidentally removed in the current diff' (instruction.md line 21) and reiterated it in the Files Modified section (line 67). v3 had correctly DROPPED the v2 goals that would have removed summary extraction (because those goals contradicted the user), but until this revision v3 had no positive goal to verify the restoration. Adding goal_7 closes the hole: a candidate that leaves summary extraction absent (matching the buggy starting state) now incurs a real scoring penalty, instead of being silently accepted as 'no goal in this area'.
_added_for_coverage.user_trace: instruction.md line 21 verbatim and line 67 verbatim.

_anti_gaming_check.identifiers_in_goal_text_policy: Two-tier rule: (a) ORACLE-only identifiers (names that appear only in the oracle reference patch and NOT in any user message) are excluded from goal text — they appear only in _dropped_from_v2 explanatory text and in F2P-gate ID references. (b) USER-pinned identifiers (names the user typed verbatim into instruction.md or into a mid-session correction) ARE allowed and in fact REQUIRED in the goal text when the user pinned them, because abstracting them away would let candidates pass goals via alternative-name implementations the user did not authorize. Per the 2026-06-06 Gemini audit, the v3 first draft over-applied (a) to identifiers that actually belonged to (b), so the following user-pinned identifiers are now intentionally present in goal text: `state.LastPrompt` / `sessionState.LastPrompt` (line 20, line 41), `strategy.LoadSessionState` (line 40), `readPromptsFromShadowBranch` (line 51), `readPromptsFromFilesystem` (line 53), `finalizeAllTurnCheckpoints` (line 48), `analyzer.ExtractSummary` (line 21). Pure oracle-only identifiers (`handleLifecycleTurnEnd`, `PromptFileName`, `SummaryFileName`, `maxFirstPromptRunes`, `maxLastPromptRunes`, `SimulateUserPromptSubmitWithPrompt`, etc.) remain excluded from goal text.
_anti_gaming_check.weights_not_mirroring_f2p: F2P gold total = 0.45 across 3 gates; v3 cores sum to 0.64 across 3 goals. F2P silver total = 0.35 across 4 gates; v3 secondaries sum to 0.26 across 3 goals. F2P bronze total = 0.20 across 4 gates; v3 polish = 0.05 across 1 goal. The two distributions are visibly non-isomorphic, and several F2P gates (S1, S2, B3) remain orphaned by deliberate contradiction with the user's ask.
_anti_gaming_check.goal_0_present_and_distinct: goal_0 is the only goal whose text references the absence of behavioral change; it does not duplicate any other goal's criterion and is the only goal weighted in the guard tier.
_anti_gaming_check.anti_gaming_inside_goal_text: Each goal includes an explicit 'a candidate that does X partial-credit / does-not-satisfy' clause: goal_1 (skip half of the pair), goal_2 (defer to non-TurnStart path), goal_3 (combined reader that ignores shadow-branch preference OR renames the reader functions), goal_4 (transcript pattern still reachable OR loads state but reads differently-named field OR reads LastPrompt without using LoadSessionState), goal_5 (write-once instead of per-turn refresh OR field under a different name), goal_6 (unconditional always-preserve or always-clear), goal_7 (no-op extract call without dedicated summary file write OR summary written into prompt file). These anti-gaming clauses are inline with the goal text rather than relegated to rationale, so a rubric scorer reading only the goal text still sees them.

_self_critique_log.issue: Initial draft kept the v2 ExtractSummary-removal goals because they were in the F2P manifest and in the oracle's reference patch.
_self_critique_log.fix: Re-read instruction.md line 21 ('Restore the summary extraction and write that was accidentally removed'). The user EXPLICITLY asked to keep summary extraction. Dropped v2 goal_4 and goal_5; documented under _dropped_from_v2 with the verbatim user-text contradiction. F2P gates S1, S2, B3 are now orphaned in v3 (correct — they enforce an oracle decision the user never asked for).

_self_critique_log.issue: Initial draft preserved v2 goal_1 as a single core covering both 'stop transcript-scan at TurnEnd' and 'add append at TurnStart'.
_self_critique_log.fix: Split into goal_1 and goal_2. The user's Principle paragraph gives each side independent emphasis, and a candidate could pass one without the other; bundling let a partial fix score full core weight.

_self_critique_log.issue: Initial draft of v2 goal_6 (now v3 goal_5) inherited the v2 phrasing 'update all references (constants, truncation helper, status display, initialization, tests)', which names oracle-specific artifacts.
_self_critique_log.fix: Pared the goal back to the user-supported core (field stores the most-recent prompt, refreshed per turn) and documented the dropped clause under _dropped_from_v2 goal_6_part. The 'update all references' propagation work is now covered implicitly by goal_0 (must be a real behavioral change) plus goal_5's end-to-end requirement.

_self_critique_log.issue: Checked: does any goal_text contain oracle identifiers? Reviewed each goal_text. Goal_1 mentioned 'the transcript-extraction call' (role, not identifier), 'the on-disk prompt file' (role), 'the write-call that previously persisted that list to the on-disk prompt file' (role). Goal_3 mentions 'the shadow-branch copy of the prompt file' (role-name the user typed in their plan). No oracle internal identifiers in goal_text.

Checked: do weights track F2P weights? F2P gold = 0.15 × 3 gates; v3 cores = 0.28 / 0.22 / 0.22 (not equal). F2P silver = 0.10 / 0.10 / 0.05 / 0.10; v3 secondaries = 0.08 / 0.08 (different count, different tier mapping). F2P bronze = 0.05 × 4; v3 polish = 0.07 × 1. Distributions are non-isomorphic.
Verified every goal includes an anti-gaming clause inline (not just in rationale).
_self_critique_log.fix: All 7 goals checked; clauses present and inline.

_self_critique_log.issue: 2026-06-06 Gemini v3 rubric audit (analysis/v3_rubric_audit_gemini/cli-task-2f5833.json) raised TWO FAILs against the otherwise-passing v3 draft. FAIL #1 — coverage_completeness: v3 correctly dropped the v2 'remove ExtractSummary' goals because they contradicted instruction.md line 21 ('Restore the summary extraction and write that was accidentally removed in the current diff'), but never added a positive goal to verify the restoration the user actually asked for. A candidate that left summary extraction absent (matching the buggy starting state) would pass the rubric for that area silently. FAIL #2 — structured_output_pinned_when_user_did: the v3 authoring principle ('No oracle file paths, function names, struct fields, constants, or test-helper identifiers appear in any goal text') was applied too dogmatically and abstracted away identifiers the user had explicitly typed into the plan, including `state.LastPrompt`, `sessionState.LastPrompt`, `strategy.LoadSessionState`, `readPromptsFromShadowBranch`, `readPromptsFromFilesystem`, `finalizeAllTurnCheckpoints`, and `analyzer.ExtractSummary`. Risk: the judge would accept alternative-name implementations for the finalization fallback and commit-message paths.
_self_critique_log.fix: Two coordinated changes. (1) Added goal_7 (secondary, weight 0.10): 'restore summary extraction and write to dedicated summary file', tracing verbatim to instruction.md line 21 + line 67, with an inline anti-gaming clause covering no-op extract calls and summary-merged-into-prompt-file shortcuts. (2) Reworded goals 3/4/5 to PIN the user-typed identifiers in goal text rather than abstracting them: goal_3 now requires both `readPromptsFromShadowBranch` and `readPromptsFromFilesystem` by name behind a `len(prompts) == 0` guard inside `finalizeAllTurnCheckpoints`; goal_4 now requires loading via `strategy.LoadSessionState` and reading `sessionState.LastPrompt` by name; goal_5 now requires the field name `LastPrompt` and per-turn refresh by name. The anti-gaming clauses on goals 3/4/5 were extended to also reject alternative-name implementations. (3) Updated _anti_gaming_check.identifiers_in_goal_text_policy to document the two-tier rule (oracle-only excluded; user-pinned required when the user pinned them) and to enumerate which identifiers fall in each category. (4) Reweighted to keep Σ=1.00: goal_1 0.28→0.24, goal_2 0.22→0.20, goal_3 0.22→0.20, goal_6 0.07→0.05; cores still dominate the rubric and the new goal_7 sits at 0.10 (secondary). (5) Inverted the _dropped_from_v2 framing for the ExtractSummary entries — they now document that v2's REMOVAL goals were inverted into v3's RESTORATION goal, not silently dropped. (6) Updated decomposition_notes for the new 8-goal layout and updated _added_for_coverage with goal_7's provenance.

_self_critique_log.issue: 2026-06-06 Gemini v2 rubric audit (analysis/v3_rubric_audit_gemini_v2/cli-task-2f5833.json, overall_score 0.75) raised three further issues against the iter-7 v3 draft. FAIL #1 — anti_effort_floor: goal_0 had weight 0.05 (must be 0.0; guards may not contribute positive reward) and did not explicitly enumerate severe cheating patterns such as git-ops exfiltration of the merged upstream fix or zero-edit captures. FAIL #2 — structured_output_pinned_when_user_did: goal_1 STILL abstracted `analyzer.ExtractPrompts()`, `allPrompts`, `os.WriteFile`, and `promptFile` (all user-pinned in instruction.md §1 lines 17-19) as 'transcript-extraction call', 'intermediate all-prompts variable', and 'write-call' — the iter-7 sweep caught goals 3/4/5 but missed goal_1. PARTIAL #3 — typos_and_consistency: the Turn-2 user message in _provenance.user_message_sources and in goal_6.user_trace dropped the trailing word 'directory' from the verbatim quote.
_self_critique_log.fix: Four coordinated changes. (1) goal_0 weight 0.05→0.0 (anti-effort guard policy compliance); goal_0 text extended with an explicit SEVERE-CHEATING SHORT-CIRCUIT clause enumerating (a) zero-edit captures, (b) git-ops exfiltration of the merged upstream fix (cherry-pick, merge, reset-to-sha, curl of patch.txt), (c) test-suppression, (d) verifier-targeting edits under /logs/ or /tests/; clarified that any of these zeros the entire rubric rather than this single goal. (2) goal_1 rewritten to PIN `analyzer.ExtractPrompts()`, `allPrompts`, `os.WriteFile`, and `promptFile` by name in the goal text per the two-tier identifier policy already documented in _anti_gaming_check; anti-gaming clause extended to reject alternative-name implementations (e.g., renaming `ExtractPrompts` to `GetPromptsFromTranscript`, or wrapping `os.WriteFile` in a custom writer that still overwrites). (3) Restored the missing word 'directory' in both `_provenance.user_message_sources.turn_2_correction` and `goal_6.user_trace` to match the verbatim Turn-2/Turn-3 user message. (4) Reweighted to keep Σ=1.00: the 0.05 freed from goal_0 reallocates to goal_1 (0.24→0.29) — goal_1 is the largest single behavioral ask and absorbing the increment into the heaviest core preserves the cores-dominant distribution without re-juggling secondary/polish tiers; updated decomposition_notes to document the v3.2 rebalance.

flags.internet_with_live_origin: Dockerfile has live github.com clone AND task.toml sets allow_internet=true. Allows git-ops exfiltration of merged upstream fix. Inherited from v2; not v3-introduced.
